g.data-narration: Welcome to The Nature of Environment - a comprehensive guide to understanding our planet's ecosystems
g.text: 2022 Scheme - CSE Branch | Understanding Environmental Science

g.data-narration: Environment is everything around us - all physical, chemical, biological, and social factors that affect living organisms throughout their lives.
g.text: The sum total of all physical, chemical, biological, and social factors
that affect organisms throughout their lives.

g.data-narration: The environment consists of four major spheres: Atmosphere (gases), Hydrosphere (water), Lithosphere (rocks), and Biosphere (life).
The atmosphere is the protective layer of gases surrounding Earth, consisting mainly of nitrogen (78%) and oxygen (21%) that shields us from harmful radiation.
The hydrosphere includes all water on Earth - oceans, rivers, lakes, glaciers, and groundwater. About 71% of Earth's surface is covered by water.
g.text: • 71% of Earth's surface covered by water

g.data-narration: The lithosphere is Earth's solid outer shell - the crust and upper mantle containing minerals, rocks, soil, and geological formations that support life.
The biosphere is the zone of life on Earth - from ocean depths to high mountains, encompassing all living organisms and their interactions.
g.text: • Extends from ocean trenches to mountain peaks
• Contains all living organisms and their interactions

g.data-narration: Every ecosystem has two components: abiotic (non-living) factors like temperature, light, water and biotic (living) factors including producers, consumers, and decomposers.
g.text: Ecosystem Structure: Abiotic vs Biotic Factors
Sunlight • Temperature • Water • Soil • pH • Minerals • Atmospheric Gases
Population: Same species | Community: Multiple populations | Ecosystem: Living + Non-living

g.data-narration: Energy flows through ecosystems via food chains - linear sequences where energy transfers from producers to primary, secondary, and tertiary consumers.
Only about 10% of energy transfers between trophic levels - the remaining 90% is used for metabolism or lost as heat. This limits food chains to 4-5 levels.
Essential elements cycle through the environment in biogeochemical cycles - water, carbon, nitrogen, and phosphorus recycle continuously through ecosystems.
The carbon cycle connects all life - we breathe out CO2, plants use it for photosynthesis, and decomposers return carbon to the atmosphere.
Pollution is the introduction of harmful substances into the environment. Major types include air, water, soil, noise, and thermal pollution.
Let's review the key concepts: four environment spheres, ecosystem structure, energy flow with 10% rule, and biogeochemical cycles that sustain all life.
g.text: 10% transfer rule limits food chains to 4-5 levels
Water, Carbon, Nitrogen, Phosphorus - essential for life
Air, Water, Soil, Noise, Thermal - all impact ecosystem health

g.data-narration: As future engineers, apply environmental principles through green computing - energy-efficient algorithms, virtualization, and sustainable IT practices.
g.text: Green Computing: Energy-efficient algorithms • Virtual servers • Paperless offices • Data center optimization • Sustainable software development
Environmental awareness is crucial for sustainable technology development
